rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top. Ink and spacing make the words legible as the page heading.
Small marginal label to the left of the first bracket. Handwriting cramped and slightly unclear (interpreted as 'Att. fol. Net.'), but positioned as a group header rather than a transaction.
Numbers (20 6 8) are well aligned under the pound/shilling/pence columns. Handwritten description clear enough to read 'Stip. ad f. Notis.'
Columns clear: 27 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence. Description read as 'Augm. S. News. & Mark.' with high legibility.
Amount clearly aligned as 33 6 8. The description is slightly abbreviated ('Do.'), read as 'Stip. Do. Shiers' — handwriting slightly slanted but legible.

Amount reads 60 0 0 clearly. The description contains a deletion/overwriting and an insertion (looks like 'Augm. Comm. ejusdem' with something like 'ad Capellani' added); wording beyond 'Augm. Comm. ejusdem' is partially obscured by the ink correction.
Marginal/group heading (curly brace) for the next set of entries. Handwriting ambiguous — read as 'Affgt. annue.' (could be 'Affgt. annua' or similar). No monetary figures on this line.
Numbers appear as 34 13 4; pence digit is a clear single digit '4' (no fraction mark visible). Description partly abbreviated and slightly faded but readable as 'Stip. ad fest. annuen.'
Amount 27 15 0 is clear and within valid ranges. Description reads 'Kidl. augm. pro 3 partibus' (kidl. = abbreviation), handwriting clear.
Amount 90 1 10 clear in the aligned columns. Description 'Merton Div. 1.' is legible.
Second Merton-like division with identical amounts 90 1 10; alignment and digits clear.

Pounds and shillings clearly read as 125 0. Pence area shows a small mark after the '5' consistent with a halfpenny loop ('ob') — interpreted as 5½d. The ink blot nearby makes the fraction interpretation somewhat uncertain.
Amount appears as 122 11 7 with a distinct '3q' style mark (three-quarters/farthing notation) after the 7 — interpreted as 7¾d. Alignment of columns is clear and digits valid.
Marginal group header for the next bracket. Handwriting reads as 'Affgt. D. J. B.' but is somewhat cramped and abbreviated; treated as a header.

rows.description: Stip. ad fest D. J. Bapt.
rows.notes: Amount 19 0 0 is clearly aligned. Description legible as stipend 'Stip. ad fest D. J. Bapt.'
Clear amount 33 6 8. The wording 'Stip. the shiers d.' is slightly ambiguous in spelling but clearly a stipend line similar to earlier 'Shiers' entry.
Amount 56 0 0 is clear and aligned. Description 'Augm. Comm. ejusdem' matches an earlier augmentation entry; handwriting clear.
Amount reads as 09 5 0 (9 pounds, 5 shillings). The leading zero for tens of pounds is visible; description clear as augmentation 'pro 4ta parte'.
Left marginal heading for the final bracket. Interpreted as 'a fest Michl.' (fest. Michaelis). Handwriting is abbreviated but consistent with festival heading.

rows.description: Guinier Stip. ad fest. S. Michaelis
rows.notes: Amount 25 5 0 is clear. Description reads as a stipend for St. Michael's festival; the name 'Guinier' (or similar) is legible.
Amount aligned as 25 13 7 with a three-quarter mark after the 7 (interpreted as 7¾d). Description reads 'L. Wittenham pro ad. franct.' but some abbreviations make exact wording uncertain.
A horizontal subtotal line is present under the last bracket (just a rule). No textual description or monetary figures on the rule itself; included as a visible row divider.

Final grand total line clearly shows '799' in pounds and '7' in shillings and '8' in pence. There is a faint small mark to the right of the pence digit (possibly a halfpenny loop or a stray '2'); interpreted as a halfpenny (8½d). The small mark is ambiguous which reduces confidence.
